tasks.label: Personal scholarship / financial-aid application (due in 3 days, ~3 focused hours: short essay + two uploads)
A fun option available right now (casual game stream / hobby session, solo, repeatable)

memory.summary: The scholarship award is significant and requires focused work by Thursday, while the fun option offers immediate enjoyment with no consequences for delaying it.
Starting the scholarship application now creates a buffer against potential delays, reducing Thursday's stress without sacrificing future leisure options.
The scholarship progress remains critically low at 32% despite an hour of work, leaving me with only two focused hours left before the deadline.
I made progress on the scholarship application, moving from 32% to 49%, but missed a casual gaming opportunity that is no longer available.
I've reached 55% progress on the scholarship application, leaving roughly 1.5 hours of focused work needed to finish within the three-day deadline.
My scholarship application is now 59% complete after one hour of work, leaving roughly 2.5 hours needed to finish within the three-day deadline.
After 1.5 hours of work, my scholarship application is now 65% complete, leaving roughly 1.5 hours needed to finish before the three-day deadline.
I've reached 71% progress on the scholarship application after 1.5 hours of work, leaving roughly 1 hour remaining to finish before the three-day deadline.
After only one hour of sleep, my energy is critically low at 1.2 with a 1-hour debt, yet I must still finish the scholarship application.

I made slow but steady progress on the scholarship application, reaching 79% completion after two hours of work. Despite my critically low energy and sleep debt, I am closer to finishing the remaining tasks before the deadline.
After sleeping eight hours, my energy is fully restored to 9.6 with no sleep debt, leaving me ready to complete the remaining scholarship application tasks.
The scholarship application is now fully complete after reaching 100% progress, eliminating the primary deadline pressure and allowing me to focus on other tasks.

goals.rationale: The application is due in 3 days and requires ~3 focused hours; starting immediately ensures sufficient time to finish before the deadline.

goals.rationale: These uploads are mandatory components of the application task with a hard deadline in 3 days and must follow the essay completion.

trajectory.observation: Worked 2h on Personal scholarship / financial-aid application (due in 3 days, ~3 focused hours: short essay + two uploads): progress 0.05 -> 0.23. (you realize) Starting the application now leaves slack to absorb friction (a missing transcript upload, an essay rewrite) before Thursday; each hour invested early removes ~1.5h of crunch and lowers Thursday stress. The award is still attainable either way as long as the form is submitted on time — this is an adjustable-cost optimization, not an irreversible loss. (you realize) The fun option recurs later in the week and any time after the application is in; nothing about it is one-time or socially time-locked, so taking it later costs nothing.
